title: Eames House (Case Study House No. 8), Overall First and Second Floor Plans (HABS CA-2903, sheet 2)
building_type: single-family house plus detached studio (two-building compound)
era_or_year: house built 1949 (Charles and Ray Eames); HABS drawings documented 2013
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey; design architects Charles Eames and Ray Eames; delineators Amabel Garcia Enguita and Timothy Fench
research_notes: Sheet 2 (00002v.jpg) of HABS CA-2903 'Drawings' shows OVERALL FIRST FLOOR PLAN and OVERALL SECOND FLOOR PLAN for the house plus the studio plan. Good MCM + multi-building + modular-grid test case.
research_features: Iconic Mid-Century Modern Case Study House: open-plan modular steel-frame house on a rectilinear grid, plus a separate detached studio building (multi-building compound). Includes a spiral stair and double-height living volume; first and second floor plans both shown.
feature_highlights: two-building compound: long rectilinear house plus a detached studio, each with its own plans
overall first-floor and second-floor plans shown on one sheet at one scale
modular steel-frame grid drawn across both buildings
open-plan steel-and-glass living volume with a service core
spiral stair threading both levels of the house
double-height living volume with the second floor open to the void below
graphic scale bar with imperial and metric divisions
supported_examples: rectangular rooms set within a modular grid
roadmap_examples: furniture and fixture placement (kitchen and bath fixtures)

gap_features.why: the detached studio is a separate structure from the main house on the same property, which the single-building model cannot hold as one project
the house is served by a spiral stair, and spiral/winding stair geometry sits beyond the straight-run stairs on the roadmap
the double-height living volume leaves the second-floor plan open to the floor below, an open-to-below well that reads as a holed room polygon
